top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 101, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
65ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 101 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
6,600ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Gran Fondo San Diego's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before April.
At Gran Fondo San Diego, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
Gran Fondo San Diego's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 101 miles.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Gran Fondo San Diego. Train it until it feels boring.
101 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
6,600ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 6,600ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 101 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.heat: April in San Diego, California, USA can bring heat. Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Gran Fondo San Diego. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for Gran Fondo San Diego’s 101 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Gran Fondo San Diego. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.
race_overlay.terrain: Mixed terrain at Gran Fondo San Diego requires surface adaptability. Include weekly rides on mixed terrain to build confidence.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Endurance to prepare you for 101 miles of mixed terrain in San Diego, California, USA.
